channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: A Post-Commit Hook Told Me to Rewrite 8 Pushed Commits to Fix "Unverified." I Said No.
item.description: I run a scheduled agent that publishes to DEV.to twice a day. After one of its runs, a stop hook fired and printed something that looked, at first glance, like a helpful lint warning: 8 commits on...

item.title: Exploring the Deep Learning Library in Modern Computer Vision
item.description: Picking the right deep learning library shapes almost everything about a computer vision project, from how fast you can prototype a model to how painful it is to ship one into production. Two...

item.title: The Simplex Method, Explained Like an Algorithm (with a Free Step-by-Step Solver)
item.description: If you have written any optimization code, you have met linear programming even if nobody called it that. "Maximize output without blowing the resource budget" is an LP problem, and the classic...

item.title: What Is Load Balancing? A Beginner's Guide to Faster and More Reliable Applications
item.description: Imagine you're standing in line at a supermarket with only one cashier open. The queue is long, and everyone has to wait. Now imagine the store opens four more checkout counters. Customers are...

item.title: Your incident postmortems aren't investigations; they're fan fiction.
item.description: I’ve seen enough postmortems to know that a large percentage of them are essentially polite fiction. We sit in a meeting, everyone is exhausted from the outage, and we agree on a narrative. We...

item.title: FROST-SOP V6.1.0 工程实践：从初始化流水线看「零门槛上手」
item.description: FROST-SOP V6.1.0 工程实践：从初始化流水线看「零门槛上手」 作者：神通说 日期：2026-07-21 主题：周二·SOP工程 | V6.1.0 新特性深度解析 开篇：一个让所有开发者头疼的问题 想象这个场景： 你刚克隆了一个开源项目，兴冲冲地跑起来，结果： ModuleNotFoundError: No module named xxx ImportError: DLL...

item.title: Subtitle readability: why a valid file can still be unreadable
item.description: I publish the same scripted show in five languages: Spanish, English, Japanese, German, and Chinese. Every episode ships with subtitles, and every language has taught me the same lesson from a...

item.title: projports: find every port your project declares
item.description: Every multi-service repo I touch has the same onboarding failure mode: clone, run the happy-path start command, get EADDRINUSE or a silent hang because Postgres is mapped to 5433 in Compose and...

item.title: openFDA Adverse Event API: Paging Past 25,000 and Flattening
item.description: You wrote a loop against api.fda.gov/drug/event.json, incremented skip, and at 25,001 got {"code":"BAD_REQUEST","message":"Skip value must 25000 or less."} (yes, the API's own message is missing...

item.title: Salesforce Flow Deletion
item.description: Ever been blocked by "Cannot delete field referenced in Flow" because of dozens of old Flow versions? I got tired of deleting them one by one, so I built an unlocked package to automate it....

item.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
item.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

item.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
item.description: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

item.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
item.description: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound...

item.title: The FCC is planning to retroactively ban disguised DJI gadgets
item.description: Last October, we told you how the FCC had given itself the power to retroactively ban gadgets that have already received its approval to be imported and sold in the United States. Now, the FCC's...

item.title: The Odyssey turned me into an IMAX believer
item.description: After seeing Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey for the first time early last week, I came away impressed, but somewhat conflicted about two of the film's more fantastical set pieces. While those...

item.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
item.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

item.title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
item.description: Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. So what happens when a company...

item.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
item.description: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

item.title: Get Borderlands 3, Risk of Rain 2 and 13 other great PC games for $15
item.description: The aptly-named “2K Megahits 2026 Bundle” from Humble includes 15 Steam games for $15. It’s a smattering of the publisher 2K’s biggest games of the 2010s, including BioShock Infinite, Risk of Rain...

item.title: The PlayStation replica ornament is an homage to a great, yet fragile console
item.description: You probably know the signature PlayStation boot sound. Did you know that it's technically a multi-part chime? There's the synthy section where "Sony Computer Entertainment" shows onscreen with a...

item.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
item.description: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

item.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
item.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.

item.title: Ford’s $30,000 electric truck: all the news about the company’s big EV re-do
item.description: The end of the Ford F-150 Lightning was also the start of a new era for the automaker. After failing to capture the market with the electric version of its popular F-Series truck, Ford is going...

item.title: FakeGit Campaign Uses 7,600 GitHub Repositories to Spread SmartLoader Malware
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered nearly 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories, out of which more than 800 pose as artificial intelligence (AI) skills or Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers...

item.title: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Over the weekend, several current and former advisors to...

item.title: Judge pauses Paramount’s attempt to buy Warner Bros. Discovery
item.description: A judge partially granted the request from a dozen state attorneys general to temporarily place the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery on hold, as reported by Variety and...

item.title: Exposed Server Reveals AI-Assisted Phishing Toolkit Behind WebDAV Malware Campaign
item.description: A malware operator left its delivery server wide open, and Rapid7 pulled down the whole toolkit: 1,048 files spanning lure templates, filename-spoofing tests, execution experiments, droppers,...

item.title: LG’s glossy OLED gaming monitor is rare to find under $400
item.description: If you’ve been thinking about upgrading your gaming monitor, LG’s 27-inch 27GX704A-B pairs a glossy WOLED panel with a fast refresh rate, and it’s currently on sale for $379.99 (about $70 off) at...

item.title: LG’s monitors come with an unwanted addition for Windows: McAfee pop-up ads
item.description: A video from Gamers Nexus explains how, after connecting a new LG UltraGear monitor to a PC running Windows 11 for the first time, Windows Update is silently installing LG driver updates and the...

item.title: Introducing Cosmos 3 Edge
